content.problemStatement:
You are an administrator of a popular quiz website. Every day, you hold a quiz on the website.

There are NNN users (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM admins (numbered N+1N+1N+1 through N+MN+MN+M). For each quiz, each user is allowed to attempt it at most once. Sometimes, admins attempt the quizzes for testing purposes too; an admin may attempt a quiz any number of times.

On a certain day, the quiz was attempted KKK times. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§K1 \le i \le K1‚â§i‚â§K), the iii-th of these attempts was made by the person with number LiL_iLi‚Äã. Find all the users who attempted the quiz more than once, in order to disqualify them.
constraints: 1‚â§Li‚â§N+M1 \leq L_i \leq N + M1‚â§Li‚Äã‚â§N+M for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
The second line contains KKK space-separated integers L1,L2,‚Ä¶,LKL_1, L_2, \ldots, L_KL1‚Äã,L2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,LK‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing an integer DDD denoting the number of users who should be disqualified, followed by a space and DDD space-separated integers denoting their numbers in ascending order.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Since there is only a single attempt during the whole day, there cannot be any user who made more than one attempt.
Example case 2: User 111 attempted the quiz twice.
Example case 3: Users 222 and 555 attempted the quiz multiple times. The admin with number 101010 also attempted the quiz multiple times, but admins are allowed to do that.
